name: What types of projects do general contractors usually handle in this area?
headline: What types of projects do general contractors usually handle in this area?

mainEntity.name: What types of projects do general contractors usually handle in this area?
mainEntity.text: What types of projects do general contractors usually handle in this area?

acceptedAnswer.text: In Pacifica, Daly City, San Bruno, Hillsborough, and Half Moon Bay, homeowners commonly hire general contractors for: - Kitchen remodels: new cabinets, countertops, layouts, and lighting - Bathroom remodels: tile showers, new tubs, vanities, and ventilation - Additions: extra bedrooms, family rooms, or second-story expansions - Garage conversions or ADUs (in-law units) - Decks, balconies, and outdoor living spaces - Window and door replacement, especially for better energy efficiency - Siding, stucco, and exterior repairs - Structural repairs: foundations, framing, and seismic upgrades In coastal neighborhoods, people also hire contractors to deal with moisture and salt-air damage, replace old windows and doors, and improve drainage around the house. A good general contractor will help you decide which projects should come first based on safety, leaks, and structural issues versus cosmetic upgrades. For example, it’s usually smart to fix a leaking roof or unstable deck before spending money on new flooring. One common mistake is tackling big jobs in the wrong order or hiring separate trades without a main coordinator. That can lead to redoing work or extra costs. Having a general contractor manage the sequence and the different trades helps keep things organized and reduces stress.
